question.text: Investors in an AIF scheme shall have rights pro-rata to:

answer.text: Their commitment amount relative to other investors
The number of units they hold regardless of value
Their seniority as investors (first come, first served)
Equal rights regardless of investment amount

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Investors in an AIF scheme shall have rights pro-rata to their commitments to the scheme, in each investment and in distribution of proceeds. This means rights are proportional to the amount committed, ensuring equitable treatment based on economic contribution. **The Trap:** Differential rights may be offered to select investors without affecting others' interests. Also, LVF investors are exempt from pari-passu requirements. **Cross-Exam Context:** Pro-rata investor rights are a fundamental investor protection provision tested in XIX-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Pro-rata rights ensure fair treatment. If a fund distributes INR 100 crore and your client contributed 5% of the corpus, they should receive INR 5 crore (subject to waterfall provisions). Check the PPM to understand any exceptions to pro-rata treatment.
